summary.intent: Address 6 distinct findings from a Codex automated code review of the DateRangeField component, covering prop sync, stale value clearing, cross-field bounds, hidden input handling, and invalidity computation.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings fixed, 8+ new tests added, 100% line/function coverage restored, clippy clean, spec updated, and xci-fast (10 gates) passed before committing and pushing.

repo: DateField's `clamp_date` (line 1870) clamps completed dates to min/max, so passing cross-field values as min/max to child DateFields would defeat the parent's swap logic — child fields must receive only global bounds.
`compare_within_calendar` returns `None` for dates in different calendar systems, which is the trigger condition for clearing a stale range value in `recompute_range`.
The `SyncProps` event must be explicitly excluded from the disabled guard in `transition` so prop-sync still fires when the component is disabled.
The project uses `cargo xci-fast` as a pre-push CI profile with 10 gates (~10–15 min runtime).
Spec files live at `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` and must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
`Bindable`'s controlled-sync API is used via `sync_controlled` for implementing `sync_props`.

code.finding: Two Codex P1 threads both pointed to line 402 — they were the same prop-sync finding reported twice.
`clamp_date` clamps completed dates to min/max; passing sibling-field values as bounds causes incorrect clamping — fix is to pass only global (component-level) bounds to child fields.

workflow: Codex review threads are fetched via a poll loop that exits on verdict, then all threads are read in bulk before starting fixes.
TDD rigor was applied: build first to confirm clean baseline, implement fixes, then add/update tests before re-checking coverage.
Coverage was tracked at line/function level; a single missed branch (`old == new` no-op in `on_props_changed`) required an explicit targeted test to restore 100%.
The workflow sequence was: verify findings → implement → build → test → coverage → clippy → spec sync → validate spec → CI → commit/push → reply to review threads.

friction: Two Codex P1 threads at line 402 were duplicate findings for the same issue, requiring deduplication before triaging.
Coverage dropped to 99.81% after fixes due to the `old == new` no-op branch in the new `on_props_changed` method, requiring an extra targeted test pass.
open_items: Re-trigger `@codex review` after push to confirm all 8 threads are resolved.
Two branch-closure coverage regions remain uncovered (noted as acceptable).
